title: Phenomenon 411
wikitext:
{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=2|effectiveness=1|danger=2}}
Overview
'''Phenomenon 411''', also known hereafter as Displacement, is an rendering error that happens occasionally in the Backrooms. The "generation" referenced is usually found inside of infinitely expansive levels. While an instance of '''Displacement''' is usually found during the generation of a part of a level, in other cases, it can occur during a rare instance of an organism turning away from a certain point in the Backrooms. This item or piece of environment will be altered from its original state or position. Whilst '''Displacement''' is occurring in a level, the affected area, or mass, will lose the ability to absorb, reflect, refract, or scatter light along with losing the ability to produce any pigment until the Displacement Event concluded. '''Displacement''' usually occurs during the level's manifestation if it is an infinite plane. An estimation of around a sixth of the generated level is subject to '''Displacement''' during its manifestation. An instance of '''Displacement''' is referred to as a "Displacement Event" if the instance is found abnormal enough to be significant. These are usually not recorded unless they affected a organism during the Displacement Event. Objects are recorded to be rooted in walls and floors. Objects embedded into landscaping or other objects. Trees, shrubs, flowers, along with other plants, have been recorded to be placed or lodged inside of other objects where the plant would not have sufficient nutrients to grow. When this occurs, a plant may seem to grow as if it has the proper nutrients to grow. '''Displacement''' can only occur after a level manifests if an or if an organism with sight is present. Otherwise, it cannot occur. The most common instance of post-manifest '''Displacement''' is when a viewer has seen an object and has turned around or has deliberately turned to view the object in question once more. If this happens, and the object has decided to execute '''Displacement''', the object will do one of the following: Movement of an object from it's origin point, sudden clipping into another object, sudden plausible shift in object's state (i.e: Closed bottle to opened bottle), property change such as color or size, floating, replaced with a completely different object (i.e: tin can to a light-bulb), shadow casting in odd way, deformations in objects original state, and others that may not be documented. Organisms with the ability to trigger a Displacement Event, or if an organism has the ability to feel its environment, cannot be physically changed directly by the phenomenon. Objects on an organism cannot be removed or changed if not initially originating from a level. (i.e Silver dollar placed in pocket cannot be affected whereas a couch in Level 9, if originating from the level, can be affected.) There are some exceptions to these rules that happen on occasion. Appendages or body structures of living organisms cannot be affected directly by a Displacement Event even if the organism originated from the affected level. If a person has placed an object inside of a level and does not collect it before exiting the level, it will be considered as a originating object until obtained or interacted with another organism that meet the requirements necessary. Note that a RAT Exception will occur before an organism perceives it.
Other Information
Masses are an informal term that is applied to any '''''200x200''''' feet squared (60.96 x 60.96 meters) in any infinite plane in the Backrooms. Masses are not recognized as a term. Organisms that can activate '''Displacement''' are usually all perceivable by visible or UV light, and must be living entities in the Backrooms. There are few exceptions and few exclusions in the list of organisms that can activate a Displacement Event. While '''Displacement''' cannot directly "attack" or affect organisms, it has the possibility to change an object into a hazard when triggered. This rarely occurs with only 17 incidents ever recorded the dates of these incidents will be in a list below. Displacement is believed to not be inherently dangerous, though wanderers believe the Phenomena to have a level of cognitivity. This is unconfirmed.
{| class="article-table mw-collapsible" !Date|| Incident ID||Description || Object Description |- |3/19/97||1A||Adult Male impaled in the abdomen by malformation in the teacup handle. Bled to death, struggled.|| Teacup, manifested a 20 foot long spike made of ceramic and clay. |- |11/28/97||2M||Adolescent Female severed in half by dart. Died instantaneously, no struggle. || Dart changed in motion and size that resulted in a 236 mile an hour (380/kph) velocity horizontally. Dart was 36 inches in radius and 6 feet in length. The point was ~20 inches large. |- |3/7/98||3U||Patch of snow landed on top of an explorer group. No deaths, none injured. || Patch of snow was 6 inches deep and 40x32 feet wide. |- |10/30/98||4M||Adolescent Male crushed by over-sized weight. No struggle, died instantaneously || Dumbbell of 50 pounds grew twenty it's mass which resulted in an intense weight shift. (Estimated ~1000 pounds) |- |6/1/99||5U||Child Male obtained and put on a white T-Shirt which changed to a blue T-Shirt after 30 minutes of wearing. No deaths, no injuries.|| T-Shirt of a Child, Male, Large size changed to a different hue. |- |8/17/99||6A||Adult Female suffocated in a couch after displacement had occurred. Struggled, died in ~20 minutes.|| Couch moved into the air and 40 feet eastward. |- |4/21/00||7S||Child Female had pointer and middle finger cut off by saw blade. Suffered minor injury, survived after recovery.|| Level item, presumably a brick, turned into a saw blade at the time of the person throwing it towards a window. |- |12/7/00||8A||Male Adult was incinerated by lava when drinking a bottle of water found on the level. Died after 2 minutes, no sign of struggle. || Liquid in bottle changed during consumption to molten lava, resulting in burning of organs. |- |3/5/01||9M|| Hound was crushed by a boulder that had accelerated to intense speeds. No struggle, instantaneously died. || Boulder changed in size which caused disturbance and resulted in a velocity shift which had resulted in the high speeds. |- |3/6/01||10S||Adult Male Faceling's leg was cut off by an industrial fan-blade. Suffered injuries, no blood visible, died two days later. || Fan turned on and removed it's fan blades after ~30 minutes. Fan blades kept revolving while detached. |- |11/29/01||11A||Male Child's eye was removed from the skull along with right side of the head. Metal can had turned into a pole that then impaled the child, killing them after an estimated 13 hours. No struggle.|| Metal can that had originated from the level had morphed into a large metal pole. |- |7/30/04||12U||While Female Child was swimming in the seas of Level 7, all water in the mass turned to almond water. No injuries, none dead. || Water changed composition to a different liquid. |- |9/23/05||14U||Tornado was rendered out of existence. || Clouds changed state to result in the two clashing fronts no longer existing. |- |1/3/06||15R||This incident is not allowed to be shared publicly. (Error Code: 57_ADA) || Object description is hidden, possibly due to a unexpected error or a classification of the description. |- |7/8/07||16A||Car in level 69 was manifested to no longer have a wheels, caused car to flip which penetrated the occupants. 3 different people, reassembled, were found in the crash, all dead, signs of struggle are inconclusive. || Car wheels disappeared, no axles were found on the car's wheel studs. Exhaust pipe was reported missing. Crash had exploded after recovery. |- |11/22/19||17M||Sudden enlargement of the spinach plant caused a sudden explosion in Level 0, one mutilated (Dead). || Chemical composition of the enlarged spinach plant proved anomalous and caused a nearby collection of gasoline barrels to combust. |- |6/14/21||18M|| An exception occured on a unknown level, the reporter was unable to leave the area before dying. The NESTED error occurred and the reporter immediately sent out a message the person was assumed dead after the collapse event. || Inside of a building on the level, a NESTED event occurred where the person could no longer perceive their surroundings properly. During the broadcast the reporter assumingly died before sufficient information could be retrieved. |}
Known Exceptions
Exceptions to a Displacement Event may happen whilst changes occur in a level, the list below is a description along with names dubbed to the exception. {| class="article-table mw-collapsible" !Exception Name || Description |- |EO (Exposed Organ) Displacement || An exposed or partially detached organ that is susceptible to a Displacement Event is touching a surface on the level. Can be changed accordingly to the abilities currently known of Displacement. |- |Natural Force Interference Mutation (NFIM)|| A natural force, such as gravity, wind, or general movement, may make a level mistake the force as an organism interacting with an object. A Displacement Event may occur and result in a more violent outcome than usual. The increased, or lack thereof, intensity of the responsible force seems to directly relate to the intensity of the abnormality. Less force results in less bizarre or drastically changed results but intense forces like high velocity impacts can result in complete change in matter compound, object size and color, and in some cases, the complete removal of the object from the level. |- |Non-Euclidean Simulator Tile Error Displacement (NESTED)|| In the event a Non-Euclidean plane is present along with the correct parameters for a Displacement Event to occur in a level, a NESTED exception may occur. If this happens, the affected non-euclidean area of the level will experience total cessation of life and matter. This usually causes a Collapse Event in the level for a variable amount of time. Level █████ is still undergoing a Collapse Event. See Log-411-01. ''Note: The term "Non-Euclidean plane" in this context does not refer to levels characterized by infinite expanse or irregular geometry. Instead, it refers to zones where perceived movement does not match actual spatial displacement—for example, walking forward may result in moving sideways on a higher-dimensional axis (e.g., left becomes right, up becomes down).'' |- |Level Adoption Binding (LAB)|| When the previous "owner" of an item, usually considered to be the last individual of higher cognitive ability that interacted with the item, has left the level without it on them (either via no-clipping, general exit, or fatal means) the item in question will undergo LAB. LAB is a metaphysical exception that affects an object under the conditions stated previously. LAB will cause an object to "store" itself within the level's memory and will await an organism to generate another '''mass''' of land before being placed accordingly. The item will only manifest again if applicable by the level's generation standards. |- |Recursive Anchor Triggering (RAT)|| Rarely, a critical error may occur during a Displacement Event causing two objects to effectively behave in similar fashion to a neutron star. This occurs when two objects undergo a Displacement Event at the same time and happen to produce such a dramatic effect on the level physics system to overload and become exponentially dense and immeasurably hot. While the objects in a RAT exception are extensively hot, the objects to not emit radiation or heat unless directly touched by an organism. When an organism does interact with the affected RAT objects, the exception will cease immediately along with the immediate incineration of the organism. Regardless of immortality, anomalous capabilities, or regenerative abilities. Further research is being done to investigate this matter, though this exception is extremely difficult to observe properly. |- |Observer Bias Distortion (OBD, a.k.a HFHED)|| Occasionally, two organisms may be simultaneously considered the "owner" of a single object and possess differing perceptions of its properties. If a Displacement Event occurs while both owners are within the same level, an OBD Exception may be triggered. During Observer Bias Distortion, the affected object will constantly fluctuate in state in an attempt to accommodate both owners' perspectives. These changes are often subtle, but continuous and jarring. For example: one owner perceives a glass as half full, while the other believes it to be half empty. The object will switch between these states in real time, even as the fluid volume remains perceptually static. The phenomenon is particularly prevalent when sensory perception varies significantly between the owners—such as in cases involving colorblindness. An item like a cup may shift colors (e.g. red to blue) depending on who is observing or interacting with it, and may even “glitch” between states when viewed by both parties simultaneously. This exception stops if both parties agree on a state of the object. |} More Exceptions may be properly documented if eligible or needing to be documentation.
Log 411-1-RATO
'''[START LOG]''' '''Researcher Vance:''' This is Liora Vance, a researcher for the M.E.G. foundation. We have a test subject here today that is going to assist with the observation and testing of a RAT exception in Level ████. ''[Expunged for security purposes.]'' The neutron star description is...very accurate. It's just a small mass of rapidly spinning light. Uhm...alright..let's get started I guess. '''[PAUSE]''' '''Researcher Vance:''' Subject 1 please enter the Experiment Zone. Do not interact with the instance. Please respond to the questions I ask accordingly. ''Subject 1, an adult male human, enters the EZ promptly.'' '''Researcher Vance:''' Subject 1, do you feel anything has changed? Such as the atmosphere, air pressure, or gravity? '''Subject 1''': It's heavy here, and dark. It looks like a solar eclipse, it's bright but it's dark. It's harder to move. '''Researcher Vance:''' Noted. Are you aware that there is no eclipse and that there are no extraterrestrial objects inside of this level? '''Subject 1:''' Yes. '''Researcher Vance:''' Are you experiencing any audiovisual hallucinations? Such as someone calling your name of a bright figure next to the instance? '''Subject 1:''' No...wait...yes. I can hear something...I think it's from that ball of light. I don't see anything out of the ordinary besides the instance, though. '''Researcher Vance:''' Describe the sound. '''Subject 1:''' I don't know what...I mean I do know what it's saying but it's in such a different tongue that I don't know how to replicate. Wait...it's describing colors...like red and blue...? '''Researcher Vance:''' Please tell me what you're hearing. How is it describing colors? What is it saying to describe them? '''Subject 1:''' ████████████ like ███████████████ ████ ████...████████████████████████...████. ████████████████████ a ███ █████ ██. It says blue is like ████████████ ██ and ████████████.[The transcriber began to malfunction and all audio during this time is heavily distorted. Heavy inspection has determined the language being spoken to be English. Though, the words spoken are so unintelligible that the transcriber was unable to properly put the words in the transcript.] '''Researcher Vance:''' ...what the fuck? That's...not what blue looks like at all. '''[After a pause lasting approximately 2 minutes and 12 seconds, the transcriber seemed to return to normal function.]''' '''Researcher Vance:''' Touch the instance. '''Subject 1:''' Are you trying to kill me? I'm not touching tha- '''Researcher Vance:''' ''TOUCH'' the instance, Subject 1. You're a hazard, this is protocol. [Subject interacted with the instance and was promptly incinerated by the instance. The two objects returned to a stable state. The objects were a small pill bottle and a empty backpack.] '''[END LOG]''' Researcher Vance was questioned for going against orders and lying about M.E.G. protocol. Vance was asked about the way Subject 1 described the color in question. She claimed the color was orange and said she couldn't speak the words because she couldn't find out how the subject made the verbalizations but she found herself completely comprehending what the subject was saying. She was fired from her role as "Researcher" and is currently in custody in Level 1 at a M.E.G. colony for the next decade. Why a RAT instance has the capability to communicate is not yet known. Nor is an instance's ability to describe the way a stimulus is perceived known. RAT instances are still heavily being monitored and should be avoided.
